summary: Gruber dissects a Bloomberg report claiming Apple 'hit roadblocks' in adding cellular networking to Apple Watch 2, arguing the report's framing is misleading. He contends that hardware lead times make it virtually impossible that cellular was ever seriously planned for this generation, and the present-tense headline creates a false impression of recent setbacks. He points out that Apple's holiday shipping requirements would have demanded October availability, making even a best-case December timeline a non-starter. Gruber concludes that Bloomberg had only one real fact — GPS addition — and fabricated a narrative around a feature that was almost certainly never planned.
key_points: Hardware lead times of over a year make last-minute feature additions to Apple products virtually impossible
Bloomberg's present-tense framing ('hits roadblocks') misleadingly implies recent setbacks rather than long-settled decisions
Apple's holiday quarter strategy requires October shipments at latest — a December best-case scenario would have been an automatic postponement
The report lacks any timeline on when decisions were made, which would reveal whether this was a disappointment or the plan all along
Bloomberg likely had only the GPS rumor and spun a story around a feature Apple never intended to include this generation
money_quotes: I'd bet my bottom dollar that teardowns of the new Apple Watch will not reveal places where the cellular modem and antenna would have gone.
Since when does Apple take advice from network carriers?
The whole thing feels to me like Bloomberg wanted to have a story on the new Apple Watch, but the one and only fact they had is the addition of GPS, which has been widely rumored all year.
So since they couldn't make a story out of what the new Apple Watch is going to have, they instead spun a story about a feature it almost certainly was never going to have.

If Apple wanted to ship the second generation Apple Watch this year, it was essential that they be able to begin shipments in October, perhaps as late as the first week of November at the very latest.
Consumers may well be disappointed, but within Apple I don't think there was ever any serious hope that cellular networking would arrive before the third or even fourth generation models.
key_insight: Bloomberg manufactured a narrative of Apple setbacks around a feature that hardware lead times and shipping logistics prove was almost certainly never planned for this generation.
